stimulus:
The following text is from Henry James’s 1908 novel The Reverberator. Mr. Probert is making his way across Paris on his way to a private appointment.

Before reaching the Quai d’Orsay, Mr. Probert turned aside out of the crowded boulevard, busy with the clatter of fiacres and the shuffle of porters, into the almost silent, swift current of black coupés. Under his hat, set at a slight forward angle, his hair had been combed into respectable smoothness; for his business was with a Legation. And Mr. Probert, firm as a column—a slightly pliant kind of column—advanced now along a street that might in every propriety be called retired.
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined phrase in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It qualifies an earlier description of Mr. Probert.
It emphasizes an internal struggle Mr. Probert experiences.
It contrasts Mr. Probert with his surroundings.
It reveals a private opinion Mr. Probert holds.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The underlined phrase qualifies (adds limits or conditions to) the description of Mr. Probert as “firm as a column,” adding that he is a “slightly pliant” column.

Choice B is incorrect because the passage never mentions Mr. Probert experiencing internal struggles. Choice C is incorrect because the underlined phrase modifies the description of him as a column rather than contrasting him with his surroundings. Choice D is incorrect because the underlined phrase further describes his character for the reader rather than revealing a private opinion he holds.
